mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over a 6-month period signals rising demand and inflation expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during periods of rising inflation (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decline in the unemployment rate over a 3-month period signals improving labor market conditions, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in improving labor market conditions are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over a 6-month period signals rising supply and economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during periods of rising industrial production (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in the federal funds rate and an increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) signals an easing monetary cycle with rising inflation expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls > 0.5% and CPIAUCSL rises > 1% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods with rising inflation are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the RSAFS (Retail Sales) over a 3-month period signals rising demand and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during periods of rising retail sales (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in the unemployment rate and an increase in the PAYEMS (Nonfarm Payrolls) signals improving labor market conditions and rising employment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.5% and PAYEMS rises > 1% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in improving labor market conditions are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the HOUST (Housing Starts) over a 3-month period signals rising supply and housing market activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during periods of rising housing starts (t-test p > 0.05).
